name: Nutter Butter Chocolate-Peanut Butter Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the crust by combining Nutter Butter sandwiches and melted butter. Press into bottom and up sides of a 9-inch pie plate. Chill until set.
2. Combine chocolate chips, 1 tablespoon corn syrup, and 1 tablespoon water in top of double boiler. Cook over simmering water until chocolate melts and mixture is smooth. Remove from heat and cool well.
3. In a 2-quart saucepan, combine peanut butter, 1/3 cup sugar, 4 tablespoons corn syrup, and 4 tablespoons water. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until sugar dissolves and mixture is well blended. Cool.
4. Beat egg whites with an electric mixer until foamy. Gradually add 2 tablespoons sugar, beating well after each addition. Add vanilla and continue beating until stiff, glossy peaks form when beaters are slowly lifted.
5. Fold egg white mixture into whipped cream. Then fold in peanut butter mixture.
6. Pour half of peanut butter mixture into crust. Drizzle half of chocolate mixture over filling. Top with remaining filling and drizzle remaining chocolate in parallel lines over filling.
7. Use a knife to cut across lines at 1-inch intervals. Freeze until firm, about 8 hours or overnight. Wrap securely in aluminum foil. Return to freezer and continue freezing for another 8 hours or overnight before serving.
